research_note.summary: The admitted grounded long-context pilot completed one DeepSeek V4 Pro call on the same excerpt as chunked pilots 004-006. The output hit the 1600-token cap and did not parse as emitted, but all five sections completed before truncation. Reviews split the comparison question: abstraction quality was the lab's strongest yet, while strict review found zero of four emitted line ranges support-valid even though all four claims have real support in the source.
what_worked: Judgment principle proposals preserved conditional logic, cited supporting claim ids, declared abstraction confidence, and stated their own compression limitations.
All four source-linked claims were faithful paraphrases with genuine support present in the excerpt.
Unsupported-claim reports identified real specification gaps in the source without adding unsupported material.
The model emitted line ranges only and did not emit character offsets, quote hashes, or raw source text fields.
The committed records preserve provider/model, prompt/config, source-hash, token/cost, truncation, locator-validation, and trace-storage metadata without committing raw source or provider payloads.
what_failed: The grounded contract did not fit the admitted 1600-token output cap: finish_reason=length and the as-emitted JSON did not parse, echoing the chunked pilot 001 contract-size failure.
Strict review found zero of four emitted line ranges support-valid; three were labeled exact anyway (overclaimed_exactness count 3).
Every claim was grounded but mislocated: the model described support accurately in its rationales while pointing the ranges at the wrong lines.
Locator quality regressed from 3/3 support-valid in chunked pilot 006 to 0/4 here on the same source under the same rubric.
negative_results: A single-pass contract combining judgment abstraction with line-range locator duty degraded locator precision severely on this run, while abstraction quality stayed high.
Cap pressure is a recorded confound: the model emitted against a hard output ceiling, so the locator regression cannot be fully attributed to the abstraction workload without a cap-relieved repeat.

Grounded-but-mislocated is a distinct failure mode from hallucination: claim faithfulness survived while locator pointing failed, which suggests locator assignment may belong in a separate pass rather than inside the abstraction contract.
reusable_by_other_labs: Separate the failure modes: claim faithfulness, locator pointing accuracy, and locator label honesty can move independently and should be reviewed as separate axes.
When a contract overflow truncates only the final section, a local tail repair can recover review value while the parse failure is still recorded as a contract-fit negative.
Reviewer-supplied corrected locators with locally computed offsets and hashes preserve comparison value even when every model-emitted range is rejected.
Budget contracts that add workload classes (abstraction plus locators) need output caps sized for the union, or an explicit priority order for which sections to complete first.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat high-quality abstraction prose as evidence of locator quality; this run shows they can diverge completely.

Do not re-run a combined abstraction-plus-locator contract at the same output cap expecting different structural results; any repeat needs a new admission update with either a larger cap or a split contract.
Do not compute offsets or hashes from rejected model-emitted ranges.
Do not copy raw source text, raw model output, provider payloads, prompt traces, or generated synthesis metrics into currentness docs.
research_note.evidence_disclaimer: This is a proposal-only live LLM pilot record, not validation, product evidence, strategy evidence, financial advice, live-trading authority, or architecture.
